Functions for handling QEMU transport data and connection status in Pebble firmware.

# Purpose
This header file defines the interface for handling communication between a QEMU serial driver and a system using the Pebble Protocol. It declares three functions: [`qemu_transport_handle_received_data`](<#qemu_transport_handle_received_data>), which processes incoming data from the QEMU serial driver; [`qemu_transport_set_connected`](<#qemu_transport_set_connected>), which updates the connection status to indicate whether the system is connected; and [`qemu_transport_is_connected`](<#qemu_transport_is_connected>), which checks and returns the current connection status. The file includes standard libraries `stdbool.h` and `stdint.h` to support boolean and integer data types, respectively. The use of `#pragma once` ensures the file is included only once in a single compilation, preventing duplicate definitions.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdbool.h`
- `stdint.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### qemu\_transport\_handle\_received\_data<!-- {{#callable_declaration:qemu_transport_handle_received_data}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/include/bluetooth/qemu_transport.h#L23>)

Processes received QEMU serial data.
- **Description**: Use this function to handle data received from the QEMU serial driver when the Pebble Protocol is in use. It must be called only when a session is connected, as it routes the received data to the communication session. If no session is connected, the function logs an error and does not process the data. Ensure that the session is connected before calling this function to avoid data loss.
- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: Pointer to the received data buffer. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership of the data.
    - `length`: The length of the data buffer. Must be a non-negative integer.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`qemu_transport_handle_received_data`](<../../bluetooth-fw/qemu/qemu_transport.c.md#qemu_transport_handle_received_data>)  (Implementation)


---
### qemu\_transport\_set\_connected<!-- {{#callable_declaration:qemu_transport_set_connected}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/include/bluetooth/qemu_transport.h#L26>)

Sets the connection state for the QEMU transport.
- **Description**: Use this function to update the connection status of the QEMU transport. It must be called to reflect the current connection state, either connected or disconnected. This function manages the session state and triggers events based on the connection status. Ensure that the transport is properly initialized before calling this function. It is important to note that this function is thread-safe and will lock the transport state during execution.
- **Inputs**:
    - `is_connected`: A boolean value indicating the desired connection state. Pass `true` to set the transport as connected, or `false` to set it as disconnected. The function will handle the transition and trigger necessary events.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`qemu_transport_set_connected`](<../../bluetooth-fw/qemu/qemu_transport.c.md#qemu_transport_set_connected>)  (Implementation)


---
### qemu\_transport\_is\_connected<!-- {{#callable_declaration:qemu_transport_is_connected}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/include/bluetooth/qemu_transport.h#L28>)

Checks if the QEMU transport is connected.
- **Description**: Use this function to determine if the QEMU transport session is currently active. It is useful for verifying the connection status before attempting operations that require an active session. This function does not modify any state and can be called at any time.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: Returns `true` if the QEMU transport session is active, otherwise returns `false`.
- **See Also**: [`qemu_transport_is_connected`](<../../bluetooth-fw/qemu/qemu_transport.c.md#qemu_transport_is_connected>)  (Implementation)
